supported_claims.claim: All methods rank the same 101 candidates per user under the same importer, metrics, provenance checks, and paired tests.
C-CRP decomposes uncertainty into boundary ambiguity, calibration gap, evidence support, and counterevidence, then uses risk-adjusted reranking.
C-CRP ranks first against eight official-code-level baselines across Sports, Toys, Home, and Tools on HR@5/@10/@20, NDCG@5/@10/@20, and MRR.
All 56 per-domain C-CRP-vs-baseline tests are positive and Holm-significant in each domain.
Event-level uncertainty stratifies ranking reliability in all four domains.
Several uncertainty terms are weak or redundant rather than uniformly necessary.
Validation-selected hyperparameters are stable within the pre-registered tolerance on NDCG@10.
The paper does not make a full-catalog recommender SOTA claim.

weak_claims.claim: LLM recommendation scores do not by themselves reveal decision reliability.
weak_claims.note: This is a broad motivation claim backed by the project framing and related-work discussion, not a standalone theorem or exhaustive empirical statement.

red_flag_scan.all: used only for enumerated four-domain/eight-baseline/56-test statements or explicit negations
red_flag_scan.necessary: used primarily to deny necessity claims; no unsupported necessity claim found
red_flag_scan.SOTA: only appears inside full-catalog/non-universal disclaimer wording
red_flag_scan.causal: only appears in disclaimers; no causal uncertainty effect claimed
red_flag_scan.optimal: only appears inside a non-optimality disclaimer

required_next_steps: Run final citation spot-check against Paper/references.bib before submission readiness.
Run section-level top-conference review and apply requested edits.
If future edits add new performance, novelty, or generality claims, rerun this audit.
